title: Derris nicou
intro: Derris nicou, also known as Derris elliptica, is a tropical plant species native to Southeast Asia that belongs to the family Leguminosae. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Derris nicou include isoflavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Derris nicou is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or extract, and is commonly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.

constituents: Derris nicou contains active constituents including rotenone, which is a naturally occurring insecticide and a member of the rotenone group of compounds. It is used to kill insects and other small invertebrates. Rotenone works by inhibiting the mitochondrial electron transport chain in insect cells, ultimately leading to cell death. It is also used as a piscicide to kill fish. The rotenone present in Derris nicou is highly toxic and needs to be handled with caution.
